name: Teruel – city tour circular
region_intro: Hiking around Hoya De Teruel offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by arid "Wild West" terrain, impressive canyons, and riverine environments. The region features unique geological formations, including the Rambla de Barrachina and the Hoz del Río Mijares. Mountain ranges like the Sierras de Gúdar and Javalambre contribute to varied elevations and provide a range of hiking experiences. Numerous waterfalls, such as those found along the Amanaderos de Riodeva trail, add to the natural appeal. Best hiking trails around Hoya De Teruel The most popular hiking route is Embalse del Arquillo de San Blas by the Camino Natural del Guadalaviar, an easy 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 46 minutes to complete, following the natural path of the Guadalaviar river. Another top favourite among local hikers is La Hoz del Rio Mijares – circular until the Barranco de Linares, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route explores the Mijares river gorge, offering views of its natural formations. Local hikers also love the Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through riverine environments and stunning waterfalls, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Hoya De Teruel is defined by canyons, arid "Wild West" landscapes, and mountain ranges. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Hoya De Teruel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 180 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Hoya De Teruel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Hoya De Teruel?
A: Hoya De Teruel offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 110 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Hoya De Teruel?
A: The region is known for its diverse and striking landscapes. You'll find arid, 'Wild West' terrain, impressive canyons like the Hoz del Río Mijares, and riverine environments. The area also features unique geological formations, including clay-rich soils sculpted by erosion, and trails through the Sierras de Gúdar and Javalambre mountains.

Q: Are there any hikes in Hoya De Teruel that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, Hoya De Teruel is home to several captivating waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974593688/amanaderos-de-riodeva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos de Riodeva Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that leads through riverine environments and stunning cascades. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974625544/amanaderos-waterfalls-and-gr-10-trail-from-camarena-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which also features these beautiful falls.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Hoya De Teruel?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Hoya De Teruel are easily accessible and suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974486978/embalse-del-arquillo-de-san-blas-by-the-camino-natural-del-guadalaviar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Arquillo de San Blas by the Camino Natural del Guadalaviar&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.0-mile route following the Guadalaviar river, perfect for a family outing. The Portellada Waterfall in Matarraña is also noted as ideal for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Hoya De Teruel?
A: While many trails in natural areas are generally dog-friendly, it's always recommended to check specific regulations for each route or protected area. Keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and ensure you clean up after them.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Hoya De Teruel?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes to enjoy the diverse landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981485482/la-hoz-del-rio-mijares-circular-until-the-barranco-de-linares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hoz del Rio Mijares – circular until the Barranco de Linares&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.0-mile path exploring the Mijares river gorge. For a more challenging option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976345844/pico-del-buitre-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Buitre Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 12.5-mile route.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I explore while hiking in Hoya De Teruel?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the striking 'Wild West' landscapes of the Rambla de Barrachina, or visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful spot. For mountain enthusiasts, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning views.

Q: What are some easy hiking trails for beginners in Hoya De Teruel?
A: Beginners have plenty of options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981481655/aceicuca-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aceicuca Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2-mile route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981604921/teruel-city-tour-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teruel – city tour circular&lt;/a&gt; offers an easy 2.5-mile walk within the city surroundings. These routes typically have minimal elevation gain and shorter durations.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Hoya De Teruel?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Hoya De Teruel offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976345844/pico-del-buitre-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Buitre Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 12.5-mile route with significant elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974625544/amanaderos-waterfalls-and-gr-10-trail-from-camarena-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 14.6 miles.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Hoya De Teruel?
A: The trails in Hoya De Teruel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, from the unique 'Wild West' terrain to the impressive canyons and beautiful waterfalls. The variety of routes catering to all skill levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Hoya De Teruel?
A: While Teruel city itself has public transport, reaching specific trailheads in the more remote natural areas of Hoya De Teruel often requires a car. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to smaller towns near popular hiking spots, but direct access to all trailheads via public transport may be limited.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Hoya De Teruel?
A: Most popular trailheads and natural attractions in Hoya De Teruel typically have designated parking areas, especially near villages or key points of interest. It's recommended to research specific trail starting points in advance to confirm parking availability and any potential fees.
tips: Teruel has a clear medieval flavor, you will realize when walking through its streets and discovering the impressive artistic legacy that it preserves. Its emblematic towers, the fabulous polychrome roof and the dome of its cathedral are the highest expressions of Mudejar art in Spain.

The Cathedral of Santa María de Mediavilla, also known as the Cathedral of Teruel, is a jewel of Mudejar art in Spain. Located in the Plaza de la Catedral in Teruel, Aragon, this cathedral has a rich history dating back to the 12th century.
Originally built in the Romanesque style, the cathedral has undergone several renovations over the centuries, incorporating Gothic and Mudejar elements. In 1587, it was elevated to the status of cathedral after the creation of the Diocese of Teruel. Of note are its tower, dome and roof, which have been declared World Heritage Sites by UNESCO.
The cathedral has a rectangular floor plan with three naves and a straight ambulatory. The central nave is higher than the side naves and is covered by a polychrome roof from the 13th century, considered a milestone of Mudejar art.

The Plaza del Torico is the heart of Teruel, a city in Aragon, Spain. This square is famous for its small statue of a bull, known as “El Torico,” which sits atop a column in the center of the square. The square has an oval shape and is surrounded by historic and modernist buildings, as well as bars and restaurants where you can enjoy local cuisine. 
The fountain of El Torico, dating back to 1855, is one of the most emblematic elements of the square. Legend has it that during the Reconquista, a bull guided by a star led the troops of King Alfonso II to the place where Teruel was founded.

The Old Viaduct, also known as the Fernando Hué Viaduct, is an impressive engineering work in Teruel, built in 1929. This viaduct was designed to connect the old town with the expansion area to the south, thus facilitating communication and urban development.

The Teruel Stairway is the work of José Torán and it is a neo-Mudejar construction that unites two of the most important artistic movements in Teruel, Mudejar architecture and the modernist style. It was built between 1920 and 1921, and inaugurated on June 5, 1921 in order to connect the city center with the Railway Station.
